title: Design frames manifest
description: The approval + build contract for one feature's design frames. Approving these frames approves BOTH the visual model and the build inventory (flows / components / packages), so an implementation cannot quietly invent a different architecture.

feature.description: Human-readable feature name. Shown as the card title on the frames index.

description.description: What these frames freeze, and what approving them commits to.

designSystem.description: The design system the frames are built in. Frames use tokens only — no raw hex/spacing/type.

plan.description: Optional path/link to the planning doc this feature derives from.

stamp.description: Content stamp of design/frames/<feature>/**, DERIVED by scripts/stamp-frames.mjs — never hand-edited. Binds an approval to the exact frames the approver saw; gate-frames-stamped recomputes it, and design-approval refuses to bind to a stale stamp.

entry.description: The navigable entry point for the frame set, relative to the feature dir.

frames.description: The ordered frame/flow sequence. Approval is PER FLOW: approving one entry unblocks that flow's implementation while others still iterate.

id.description: Stable flow id, matching the file stem (e.g. '01-invoice-history').

file.description: Frame file, relative to the feature dir.

route.description: The app route this flow lands on, e.g. '/billing'.

acceptanceNotes.description: The behavioural contract QA writes RED specs against. States are contract, not decoration — cover loading/empty/error and the real fail-closed cases, not just the happy path.

testHooks.description: The data-* selectors Playwright drives. These are the stable handles between frames and specs.

approved.description: Per-flow approval. Written by the design-approval workflow onto the frames PR head branch — never hand-set, never on master.

approvedBy.description: GitHub login of the approver (must be owner/CODEOWNER).

approvedAt.description: ISO-8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) or full timestamp of the approval.

contract.description: The API/type contract these frames render against — the seam to contract-first.

openapi.description: Path to the OpenAPI spec, e.g. 'services/billing-service/openapi.yaml'.

schemas.description: The OpenAPI schema components the frames bind to.

featureFlag.description: Flag gating the feature, taxonomy '<repo>.<domain>.<flag>'. Default OFF; builders audience must be targeted or prod-smoke can never see it.

build.description: The build inventory, rendered in index.html and mirrored here. Approving the frames approves this plan.

orchestrator.description: Component that orchestrates the flow, e.g. 'PasswordResetFlow'.

components.description: React components to be created. Design-system-first: missing primitives are added to the DS, not one-offed here.

packages.description: npm packages to be created/extended. Private: publishConfig → GitHub Packages, access restricted.
